simSettings.title: SIM Settings Configuration
simSettings.description: Configure the simulation environment settings for testing this hypothesis
simSettings.descriptionDetail: Configure the agent classes and environment modules that will be used to test this hypothesis in the simulation experiment. These settings define the simulation environment for validating your research hypothesis.

agentClasses.title: Agent Classes
agentClasses.description: Select the agent classes that will participate in the simulation to test this hypothesis.
agentClasses.hint: Click on a card to view full description

envModules.title: Environment Modules
envModules.description: Select the environment modules that will be used to create the simulation environment for testing this hypothesis.
envModules.hint: Click on a card to view full description

simSettings.loading: Loading agent classes and environment modules...

note.title: Note:
note.text1: The selected agent classes and environment modules define the simulation environment that will be used to test and validate this hypothesis.
note.text2: These settings determine what types of agents will participate in the simulation and what environmental conditions they will interact with.
note.text3: Changes will be saved to SIM_SETTINGS.json automatically.

chat.title: AI Assistant
chat.placeholder: Enter your question or request... (Shift+Enter for new line)

prefillParams.title: Prefill Parameters
prefillParams.introHintBody: Environment modules and agent classes from the backend, with prefill values from .agentsociety/prefill_params.json. The JSON tree here is read-only; use Open prefill_params.json to edit. Custom entries can use Test.
prefillParams.selectClass: Select a class to view its prefill parameters

classInfo.noPrefillParams: This class does not have any prefill parameters configured.

replay.noDataHint: This experiment doesn't have any recorded simulation steps. Make sure the simulation was run with replay recording enabled.

skillMarketplace.title: Skill Marketplace
skillMarketplace.subtitle: Discover and install curated skills
skillMarketplace.uninstallConfirmContent: Are you sure you want to uninstall this skill?
skillMarketplace.author: Author
skillMarketplace.installLocationHint: Choose where to install skills: workspace (current project only) or global (available for all projects)

skillManagement.title: Skill Management
skillManagement.subtitle: Agent runtime and Claude folder skills; install from marketplace; toggles apply immediately (off does not delete files). See each tab for details.
skillManagement.agentTabIntroBody: This list comes from the backend API (same as the running agent). “Shipped with runtime” are bundled with AgentSociety. “Workspace-registered” covers custom/skills, imports, and skills installed to the agent from the marketplace. Other registered sources (e.g. env) also appear under workspace-registered. The switch only toggles load at runtime; “Archive” moves the folder out of custom/skills and unregisters it without deleting sources.
skillManagement.claudeTabIntroBody: Single merged list: bundled VSIX skills first—use “Sync to workspace” to copy each into the project’s .claude/skills. Remaining rows are folder skills already on disk (workspace or user home). Full sync no longer wipes marketplace-installed skills.
skillManagement.skillMdMetaOnly: (YAML front matter only; no body to preview)
skillManagement.purgeClaudeConfirmContent: This removes “{{name}}” from disk. You cannot turn it back on from this page. To hide it temporarily, use Deactivate instead.
skillManagement.archiveAgentConfirmContent: “{{name}}” will be moved out of custom/skills into an archive folder and removed from the registry. Files are kept; restore by moving back and scanning or via the restore API.
skillManagement.claudeWorkspaceExplain: Under the opened workspace root, project-local only. Marketplace “install to Claude”, import, or sync usually writes here.
skillManagement.claudeGlobalExplain: Under your user home, shared across projects. Good for personal skills you reuse everywhere.
skillManagement.vsixTemplateExplain: Read-only copies shipped inside the extension (not a folder in your repo). Preview here; “Sync to workspace” copies them into the workspace path above.

marketplaceErr.noSourcesAgent: No Agent marketplace sources: configure `agentSkills.skillSources` (installs to custom/skills).
marketplaceErr.noSourcesClaude: No Claude marketplace sources: configure `agentSkills.claudeSkillSources` (installs to .claude/skills).

skillManagement.deleteConfirmContent: Are you sure you want to delete skill "{{name}}"? This action cannot be undone.
skillManagement.noClaudeCodeSkills: No .claude/skills folders and no VSIX templates to show
skillManagement.noMarketplaceSkills: No marketplace items (check configuration and messages above)
skillManagement.noMarketplaceSkillsAgent: No Agent marketplace items: check agentSkills.skillSources and messages above.
skillManagement.noMarketplaceSkillsClaude: No Claude marketplace items: check agentSkills.claudeSkillSources and messages above.
skillManagement.marketplaceRefreshHint: Refresh updates both marketplaces; each tab’s button opens the matching setting.
